description_long: This is a World War I-era British Mark IV tank, characterized by its rhomboid shape and continuous track system that wraps around the entire body. The vehicle is painted in olive green with prominent white and red stripes on the sides and top, and features a large, centrally mounted cannon on the roof. Both sides of the tank have sponsons with protruding machine guns, and the hull is covered in thick, riveted armor plating. The tank displays the marking 'H41' in white on both sides, along with a red and white cross insignia. The tracks are wide and robust, designed for traversing rough terrain, and the overall structure is boxy and heavily armored. The vehicle's design is utilitarian, with minimal external detailing beyond its functional components and military markings.
description: A heavily armored, olive green British Mark IV tank with full-length tracks, side-mounted sponsons, and prominent red and white military markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide tracks, central cannon, and top hatch with red and white markings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the 'H41' marking, red and white stripe, sponson with machine gun, and cross insignia.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the tracks, rear armor, and the central cannon extending forward.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with 'H41' marking, red and white stripe, sponson, and cross insignia.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One main cannon on the roof center, two side-mounted machine gun sponsons (one per side).
attribute_armor_plating: Full body coverage with thick, riveted steel armor plating; sloped and reinforced on all sides.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: Solid olive green with bold white and red stripes and cross insignia; no complex camouflage pattern.
attribute_military_markings: White 'H41' on both sides, red and white cross insignia on top and sides, red and white vertical stripes on both sides.
